text: The surgeon introduces the reusable transanal DAP report into the anus to treat a benign rectal stricture. A silicon cap is applied, allowing the use of a 10mm, 30-degree scope and a reusable curved DAPRI instrument. The benign stenosis is visible. A monocurve reusable grasping forceps is inserted through the port's orifice at the nine o'clock position, while a measuring straight grasper is inserted at the three o'clock position. The lesion is confirmed to be 2.5 centimeters away from the anal margin.

A monocurve reusable coagulating hook is inserted at a three o'clock position to begin the procedure. A circumferential tattoo is made around the rectal stenosis, followed by dissection starting with a mucosal opening at a six o'clock position, extending laterally and then anteriorly. The surgeon uses curved instruments to operate ergonomically, avoiding hand conflicts. Intraluminally, the muscle layer is gently pushed laterally with the coagulating hook, revealing the submucosal plane posteriorly.

The surgeon continues submucosal dissection cranially while preserving the mucosal layer at the bottom. The coagulating hook is used to expose muscular fibers as dissection progresses posteriorly, laterally, and anteriorly, ensuring the muscle layer remains intact.

The final view of the dissection reveals the hook introduced into the mucosal lumen, traversing the stenosis. The distal margin of the dissected mucosa is cut, and the specimen is removed through the D-port. The length of the stenosis is displayed, with the muscular layer and distal mucosal margin controlled for repair. The feasibility of suture repair is visually assessed.

The surgeon introduces a monocurve reusable needle holder through the port orifice at the three o'clock position, inserting the needle through the central port. The first suture is placed at the six o'clock position, passing through the inferior and superior edges. Intercorporeal sutures and knots are performed under ergonomic conditions. Monocurve reusable scissors are used to cut the first suture. Another suture is placed at the twelve o'clock position, followed by a suture at the two o'clock position. The final result of the closure is shown, completing the procedure.
